{"id":"harmony-4uw","title":"Implement Reticulum Interface trait and basic interface types","description":"Phase 2 continued: The Interface trait is the abstraction between Reticulum's protocol logic and physical/logical transports.

Define:
- Interface trait: send(packet), receive() → packet, mtu(), online(), bitrate()
- InterfaceMode: Full, Gateway, AccessPoint, Roaming
- IFAC (authenticated interface) support: HMAC-based interface authentication
- Basic in-memory interface for testing (loopback)

This is the boundary where sans-I/O meets real I/O. The trait must be transport-agnostic so we can later plug in TCP, UDP, serial, LoRa, Zenoh, etc. Harmony nodes will use this to connect Reticulum control plane traffic across whatever physical links are available.

Key design: the interface reports its bitrate/capacity so the node can make intelligent decisions about bandwidth allocation — prioritizing control plane traffic and being a good neighbor on constrained links.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T22:03:57Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-01T22:03:57Z"}

{"id":"harmony-0p6.1","title":"Crate bootstrap + Key expression integration","description":"Create the harmony-zenoh crate and integrate zenoh-keyexpr (no_std) for key expression matching.

Scope:
- Create harmony-zenoh crate in workspace with zenoh-keyexpr dependency (no_std, no default features)
- Define Harmony's key expression hierarchy as typed constants/builders:
  - harmony/server/{id}/channel/{id}/msg — channel messages
  - harmony/dm/{user_a}/{user_b}/msg — direct messages
  - harmony/presence/{server_id}/{user_id} — online status
  - geo/s2/{levels}/{rns_hash} — geospatial routing
  - lookup/rns/{node_hash} — distributed node lookup
- Build a subscription matching engine using KeTrees (intersects/includes operations)
- Validate key expression format at construction time
- Tests for: Harmony-specific path patterns, wildcard matching (* and **), KeTree lookups, invalid expression rejection

Depends on: nothing (first bead in chain)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:08:29Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T07:08:29Z"}

{"id":"harmony-0p6.2","title":"Message envelope + E2EE","description":"Define the Harmony message envelope with end-to-end encryption, using zenoh-protocol and zenoh-codec (no_std) for wire format.

Scope:
- Add zenoh-protocol + zenoh-codec dependencies (no_std)
- Define HarmonyEnvelope: encrypted payload + plaintext routing metadata (key expression, sequence number, encoding, timestamp)
- E2EE integration: encrypt message body with harmony-crypto ChaCha20-Poly1305; routing metadata remains in cleartext for intermediate routers
- Implement encode/decode using zenoh-codec's WCodec/RCodec traits
- Message types: Put (data), Del (tombstone), with extensible encoding field
- Tests for: envelope roundtrip, E2EE encrypt/decrypt cycle, tampered payload detection, cross-crate integration with harmony-identity

{"id":"harmony-0p6.3","title":"Session state machine (sans-I/O)","description":"Build a sans-I/O session coordinator (same pattern as harmony-reticulum's Node) that manages peer connections and resource ID mapping.

Scope:
- Session struct with SessionEvent (inbound) / SessionAction (outbound) enums
- Session lifecycle state machine: Init → Open → Active → Close
- Resource ID mapping: declare key expression ↔ ExprId for wire efficiency (avoids sending full string paths on every message)
- Peer tracking: connected peers with their declared interests and capabilities
- Keepalive timer: emit keepalive actions, detect stale peers
- Close handshake: graceful shutdown with undeclare propagation
- No actual I/O — caller drives the event loop (same as Node.handle_event())
- Tests for: handshake flow, resource ID allocation/deallocation, keepalive timeout, peer lifecycle, close handshake

Depends on: harmony-0p6.2","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:08:50Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T07:08:50Z"}

new_value: Session state machine (sans-I/O) implemented and merged in PR #13

{"id":"harmony-0p6.4","title":"Pub/Sub routing","description":"Implement publisher/subscriber primitives within the Session, with key expression tree-based message routing.

Scope:
- Publisher declaration: register intent to publish on a key expression
- Subscriber declaration: register interest in a key expression (with wildcards)
- Push message dispatch: match incoming Push messages against subscriber KeTree, deliver to matching subscribers
- Outbound Push construction: serialize and emit SendMessage actions for remote subscribers
- Interest propagation: track which peers have subscribers for which key expressions; only forward where there is interest (write-side filtering)
- Local vs remote delivery: local subscribers get direct delivery, remote subscribers get outbound messages
- Tests for: declare/undeclare lifecycle, wildcard matching, multi-subscriber dispatch, interest-based filtering, message ordering

Depends on: harmony-0p6.3","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T07:09:00Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T07:09:00Z"}

{"id":"harmony-j1d","title":"PacketHashlist: use [u8;32] instead of Vec<u8>","description":"PacketHashlist uses HashSet<Vec<u8>> but every insert is a [u8;32] converted via .to_vec(),
allocating 32 bytes on the heap for every single inbound packet (node.rs:919).

Fix: Change PacketHashlist to HashSet<[u8;32]>. One-line structural change, eliminates one
heap allocation per inbound packet with zero downside. [u8;32] is Hash+Eq and stack-allocated.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/packet_hashlist.rs, crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs:919

{"id":"harmony-y1r","title":"Trivial derive fixes: Copy for AnnounceRateConfig","description":"AnnounceRateConfig has three Copy-able fields (u64, u32, u64) but only derives Clone.
This forces .clone() at node.rs:976 on every rate-checked announce.

Fix: Add #[derive(Copy)] to AnnounceRateConfig. One-word change.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs (struct definition ~L50)

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:49Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-0d1","title":"Avoid full PathEntry clone on data packet relay hot path","description":"process_data_packet (node.rs:1107) and forward_link_request (node.rs:1218) clone the entire
PathEntry struct to escape a borrow conflict with &mut self. PathEntry includes random_blobs:
Vec<[u8;10]> (up to 64 entries = 640 bytes) plus interface_name String — but downstream code
only needs (next_hop: [u8;16], interface_name, hops: u8).

Fix: Extract only the needed fields after path_table.get() instead of cloning the full entry.
E.g. let (next_hop, iface, hops) = match self.path_table.get(&dest) { ... };

This eliminates cloning random_blobs on every forwarded data packet. Combined with the
Arc<str> interface name work (harmony-b6p), the extraction becomes entirely Copy.

Files: crates/harmony-reticulum/src/node.rs:1107, 1218

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:32Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:32Z"}

{"id":"harmony-b6p","title":"Intern interface names as Arc<str> across Node state tables","description":"Interface names (String) are cloned extensively throughout Node routing paths:
- PathEntry.interface_name, ReverseTableEntry.received/outbound_interface,
  LinkTableEntry.outbound/received_interface, AnnounceTableEntry.source_interface
- ~15 clone sites in node.rs hot paths (per-packet relay, per-announce, per-link-data)
- Real nodes have 2-10 interfaces, so the same few strings get cloned into dozens of entries

Fix: Change all interface name fields from String to Arc<str>. All clones become refcount
bumps. Pervasive change across Node, PathEntry, ReverseTableEntry, LinkTableEntry,
AnnounceTableEntry, and the NodeAction enum variants.

Affected sites (node.rs): L786, L1003, L1029, L1131, L1132, L1193, L1201, L1255, L1257,
L1270, L1316, L1381, L1383, L1391, L1396, L1530, L1538

Files: node.rs, path_table.rs (PathEntry), plus action/entry structs

Discovery: Zero-copy audit of harmony-reticulum during harmony-0p6.4 pub/sub review.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:22Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:39:22Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"harmony-ny3","title":"Arc<[u8]> for Packet.data to enable zero-copy forwarding","description":"Packet.data is Vec<u8>. Several forwarding paths clone the entire payload (up to 481 bytes)
to construct a new Packet with rewritten headers:
- route_link_proof (node.rs:1331) — per LRPROOF forward
- route_proof (node.rs:1521) — per proof relay
- process_announce_table (node.rs:780) — per announce retransmit
- send_on_interface broadcast (node.rs:631) — per interface × per broadcast

Fix: Change Packet.data from Vec<u8> to Arc<[u8]>. Forwarded packets share the original
buffer via refcount bump. NodeAction::SendOnInterface.raw could also become Arc<[u8]> so
broadcasts to N interfaces share one buffer.

This is the largest structural change in the zero-copy series. Consider whether bytes::Bytes
(standard in tokio/networking ecosystem, supports cheap subslicing) is more appropriate than
Arc<[u8]> given Harmony will eventually integrate with async runtimes.

Depends on: Consider doing harmony-j1d (PacketHashlist) and harmony-b6p (Arc<str>) first
as simpler wins.

Files: packet.rs (Packet struct), node.rs (forwarding paths, NodeAction), announce table

Scope:
- Token declaration: bind a liveliness token to a key expression (e.g., harmony/presence/server1/user42); token lifetime tied to session
- Token undeclaration: explicit removal or automatic on session close
- Liveliness subscriber: subscribe to token appears/disappears events on a key expression pattern
- Liveliness query: list currently alive tokens matching a pattern
- Timer-based expiry: detect tokens from disconnected peers via keepalive timeout
- Event emission: LivelinessAppeared / LivelinessDisappeared actions
- Tests for: token lifecycle, subscribe to wildcard patterns, disconnect detection, query current state, multiple tokens on same session

{"id":"harmony-qn0w","title":"Node Trinity: extend harmony-zenoh with namespace constants and Locality","description":"- priority: P2
- labels: node-trinity
Add the full Zenoh key expression namespace as typed constants (harmony/content/**, harmony/compute/**, harmony/workflow/**, etc.). Add Locality enum (Local/Lan/Remote) for query reply metadata. These form the shared vocabulary that all three tiers and all applications use.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md section 6.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-k5ed","title":"Node Trinity: extend harmony-content with StorageTier wrapper","description":"- priority: P2
- labels: node-trinity
Add StorageTier<B: BlobStore> wrapper that integrates ContentStore with Zenoh queryable and subscriber patterns. Declares sharded queryables on harmony/content/{prefix}/**, subscribes to harmony/content/transit/** for opportunistic caching, handles harmony/content/publish/** for explicit storage. Includes StorageBudget configuration and StorageMetrics for observability. Sans-I/O: returns actions for caller to execute.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md sections 2 and 4.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-0jk6","title":"Node Trinity: extend harmony-node to full Tier 1 + Tier 2 runtime","description":"- priority: P2
- labels: node-trinity
Transform harmony-node from identity-only CLI to the full node runtime. Instantiate Tier 1 (harmony-reticulum Node + harmony-zenoh routers) and Tier 2 (StorageTier with configurable backend). Implement the priority event loop: router drains queue completely before yielding to storage. Add configuration for storage backend selection (memory, SQLite, RocksDB) and budget sizing.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md sections 3, 4, and 7.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-x6t8","title":"Node Trinity: new harmony-compute crate (WASM execution engine)","description":"- priority: P2
- labels: node-trinity
Create harmony-compute crate with ComputeRuntime trait abstracting over WASM runtimes. Implement WasmiRuntime (interpreter, no_std, embedded). Add InstructionBudget for cooperative yielding and Checkpoint serialization for durable execution. ComputeResult enum: Complete, Yielded, NeedsIO, Failed. Sans-I/O: the runtime executes a bounded slice and returns, never blocks.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md section 5.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-nrqp","title":"Node Trinity: integrate Tier 3 compute into harmony-node event loop","description":"- priority: P3
- labels: node-trinity
Add Tier 3 (harmony-compute + harmony-workflow) to the harmony-node runtime. Register compute queryables on harmony/compute/activity/{type}. Implement the three-tier priority event loop: router drains all, then storage handles one, then compute executes one instruction slice. Add adaptive budgeting based on system load and power source.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md section 7.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-421","title":"Compute: WASM content read access via NeedsIO","description":"Allow WASM compute tasks to read immutable content from the content-addressed storage system by CID. When a WASM module emits a host call to read a CID, the runtime suspends the task (NeedsIO), resolves the CID via Tier 2 storage (local cache or network fetch), and resumes the task with the data. Since all content is immutable and auditable (CID = hash), there is no trust boundary to enforce for reads. This turns the NeedsIO path from an error into a working content resolution pipeline. Design notes in docs/plans/2026-03-04-tier3-compute-integration-design.md under 'Future: Content Read Access for WASM'.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T05:14:05Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-04T05:14:05Z"}

{"id":"harmony-hmf7","title":"Node Trinity: add wasmtime JIT runtime for desktop/server compute","description":"- priority: P3
- labels: node-trinity
Implement WasmtimeRuntime as an alternative ComputeRuntime for desktop and server nodes. JIT compilation for faster WASM execution. Feature-gated behind std (not available on no_std embedded). Auto-select wasmtime on capable hardware, wasmi on constrained devices.
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md section 5.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-awg6","title":"Node Trinity: new harmony-workflow crate (durable execution orchestration)","description":"- priority: P3
- labels: node-trinity
Create harmony-workflow crate for Temporal-inspired durable execution. Checkpoint storage and retrieval via Zenoh key expressions (harmony/workflow/{id}/checkpoint). Execution history and deterministic replay. Compute offload decision heuristics: evaluate battery level, CPU load, network latency, peer capacity, power source. ComputeHint enum (PreferLocal, PreferPowerful, LatencySensitive, DurabilityRequired).
See docs/plans/2026-03-03-node-trinity-design.md sections 5 and 7.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:37:49Z"}

{"id":"harmony-pzem","title":"Client UX: message priority levels (quiet / standard / loud)","description":"- type: feature
- priority: P2
- labels: client-ux
Implement three-tier message priority system. Quiet: dot-only, no push, optional sound for opted-in receivers. Standard: normal notification with sender's sound. Loud: breaks DND by default, distinct sound. Sender UX: Enter=standard, Ctrl+Enter=quiet, dropdown/command for loud. Receiver controls per-peer/per-community/global with policies from silent to break_dnd.
See harmony-client docs/plans/2026-03-03-chat-ux-vision-design.md section 4.
---","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:02:45Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:02:45Z"}

Implement profile extensions: 1024x1024 source + 256x256 mini avatars stored as CIDs in harmony-content CAS. Three notification sound slots (quiet/standard/loud) as CID-referenced audio files (WAV/OGG, max 500KB). Deterministic identicon fallback for users without avatars. Receiver override chain: per-peer > per-community > sender default > client default.
See harmony-client docs/plans/2026-03-03-chat-ux-vision-design.md section 3.
